prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for acrolein were computed with:

• Property: Fukui functions (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) and dual descriptors per atom; identification of the most electrophilic and most nucleophilic sites.
• Level of theory: DFT, functional B3LYP with def2-TZVP basis (tier “standard”); no density fitting (exact four-center integrals).
• Geometry provenance: fixed Cartesian coordinates taken from the user-supplied file /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/acrolein/acrolein.xyz (no re-optimization; single-point population analyses on that geometry, noted as DFT//user-provided geometry).
• Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).
• Charge = 0, multiplicity = 1 for the neutral; cation and anion states run at charges +1/−1, multiplicities 2 (doublets) per default.
• Software/backend: PySCF (via chemkit fukui-reactivity skill).
• Convergence & integrity: SCF cycles for N, N±1 states converged; integrity status = “ok”, trustworthy = true.  Live log streamed during run (path printed at launch by chemkit).
• Warnings (verbatim from engine):
  – sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
  – sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
  – common header present

Key results (Mulliken-based indices):
• Most electrophilic site: atom 4 (C, index 3) with f⁺ = 0.64232102.
• Most nucleophilic site: atom 1 (O, index 0) with f⁻ = 0.39388616.

Full per-atom values (indices 0-based):
Atom 0 (O):   f⁺ 0.40136419   f⁻ 0.39388616   dual  0.00747804
Atom 1 (C):   f⁺ 0.022255106 f⁻ 0.005988435 dual  0.016266671
Atom 2 (C):   f⁺ 0.43404673  f⁻ 0.091403097 dual  0.34264363
Atom 3 (C):   f⁺ 0.64232102  f⁻ 0.087369815 dual  0.5549512
Atom 4 (H):   f⁺ –0.1572595  f⁻ 0.085961423 dual –0.24322092
Atom 5 (H):   f⁺ –0.2072029  f⁻ 0.1856796   dual –0.39288251
Atom 6 (H):   f⁺ –0.062116996 f⁻ 0.084485809 dual –0.14660281
Atom 7 (H):   f⁺ –0.073407645 f⁻ 0.065225658 dual –0.1386333

These values allow qualitative prediction of electrophilic (highest f⁺) and nucleophilic (highest f⁻) attack sites in acrolein under the stated gas-phase, neutral singlet conditions.
